steps.title: Block the cylinder
steps.description: Lightly sketch a tall rectangle for the mug body. Add a flat oval at the top and bottom — these will become the rim and base.

steps.title: Refine the body
steps.description: Draw the two vertical sides as confident, straight lines. Long lines are smoother when you draw from the shoulder, not the wrist.

steps.title: Draw the rim
steps.description: Trace the top oval as a clean ellipse. Make sure both ends curve symmetrically — this is what sells the mug as 3D.

steps.title: Draw the base
steps.description: Trace just the front half of the bottom ellipse. The back half is hidden behind the mug — don't draw what you can't see.

steps.title: Add the handle
steps.description: Add a curved handle on one side. A flattened C-shape works well. Make sure the top and bottom of the handle attach cleanly to the body.

steps.title: Suggest the inside
steps.description: Add a smaller, parallel ellipse just inside the rim to hint at the thickness of the mug wall.
